steps.title: Understand the units
steps.content: The given rate is $1{,}800$ meters per hour per second, i.e. $1{,}800\ \tfrac{\text{m/h}}{\text{s}}$. We want feet per second squared, $\tfrac{\text{ft/s}}{\text{s}}=\text{ft/s}^2$. We need to convert meters to feet and hours to seconds.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A ($0.15$): Off by a factor of about $10$; this would result from dividing by $3.28$ and by $3600$. Eliminated.
  • B ($1.64$): Matches the computed value $\approx 1.64$ ft/s$^2$.
  • C ($9.15$): Too large; this would result from dividing by $3.28$ and by $60$. Eliminated.
  • D ($98.4$): Far too large; would come from dividing by $60$ instead of $3600$ after converting to feet. Eliminated.
That matches Choice B — 1.64.
